taskDescription: Arrange the criteria for sex determination from the bony pelvis, from the most reliable single criterion to the least reliable (AN53.3).
paragraphs: Greater sciatic notch: wide (nearly 90 degrees) in females; narrow (less than 70 degrees) in males -- the most reliable single criterion
Subpubic angle: approximately 80-85 degrees in females (rounded arch); approximately 50-60 degrees in males (V-shaped)
Pelvic inlet shape: oval/transversely wide (gynaecoid) in females; heart-shaped (android) in males
Sacrum: short, wide, and curved in females; long, narrow, and less curved in males
Obturator foramen: triangular in males; oval in females
